11th Founding Anniversary of Bahay Aruga ng Montalban 
The Municipality of Rodriguez proudly celebrates the 11th Founding Anniversary of Bahay Aruga ng Montalban, reaffirming its commitment to providing a safe, supportive, and rehabilitative halfway home for children in conflict with the law (CICL).
Through holistic care, life skills development, restorative practices, and strong partnerships with families, communities, and institutions, Bahay Aruga continues to guide every child toward personal transformation, reintegration, and responsible citizenship.
This meaningful program is implemented by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO), with the full support of the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO), strengthening collaborative efforts in promoting inclusive and child-sensitive interventions.
This initiative is made possible through the continued support of the Office of the Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan, reaffirming the municipality’s dedication to social protection and inclusive development.
The Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO), together with its team, remains committed to continuously supporting programs like Bahay Aruga that uphold care, protection, and empowerment for vulnerable sectors.
Together, we celebrate not just another year, but a continuing journey of hope, healing, and second chances for every child.
#BahayAruga
#ChildProtection
#GAD

Vermi Composting Initiative for Women
“Gulayan sa Bakuran: Tanim Ngayon, Ani Bukas” 
In celebration of National Women’s Month, the Municipality of Rodriguez successfully conducted the Vermi Composting Initiative for Women, promoting sustainable agriculture and empowering women through practical livelihood skills.
This program, initiated by the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAO) with the support of the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO), aims to encourage households—especially women—to engage in backyard gardening and organic composting, contributing to food security and environmental sustainability.
This initiative was made possible through the continued support of Municipal Mayor, Hon. Gen. Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret.), Municipal Vice Mayor, Hon. Edgardo Z. Sison, and the Sangguniang Bayan, reaffirming the municipality’s commitment to women empowerment, sustainable development, and inclusive community programs.
Through activities like this, the Municipality of Rodriguez continues to promote self-sufficiency, environmental awareness, and gender-responsive development, empowering women as key drivers of community growth.
#NationalWomensMonth
#GAD
#VermiComposting
#GulayanSaBakuran
#WomenEmpowerment

The Safe Spaces Act in the Philippines is officially known as Republic Act No. 11313, also called the “Bawal Bastos Law.” It was enacted in 2019 to protect people from gender-based sexual harassment in public spaces, workplaces, schools, and even online.
 What it covers
The law penalizes harassment in places such as:
• Streets and public areas
• Public transport
• Workplaces
• Schools
• Online platforms (social media, messaging apps)
 Examples of prohibited acts
• Catcalling, wolf-whistling, or unwanted sexual comments
• Offensive gestures or stalking
• Sharing sexual messages or images without consent
• Persistent unwanted invitations or advances
• Gender-based insults or slurs
 Key protections
• Protects all genders, not just women
• Requires institutions (schools, companies, LGUs) to create policies against harassment
• Encourages bystander intervention and reporting
 Penalties
Penalties vary depending on the severity and repetition of the offense, ranging from:
• Fines
• Community service
• Imprisonment
 Why it matters
The Safe Spaces Act promotes respect, dignity, and safety for everyone, helping create environments where people can live, work, and study without fear of harassment.
#BagongMontalban
#MGADO
#RA11313

GAD Orientation on Women’s Rights, Gender Sensitivity, and Empowerment for PDL of Montalban
The Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) successfully conducted the GAD Orientation on Women’s Rights, Gender Sensitivity, and Empowerment for the Female Persons Deprived of Liberty (PDL) at the Rodriguez Municipal Jail – Female Dormitory.
The activity aimed to strengthen awareness on women’s rights, promote gender sensitivity, and empower PDLs by equipping them with knowledge that supports dignity, self-worth, and reintegration into society.
This initiative was made possible through the continued support of Municipal Mayor, Hon. Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret. Gen.), Municipal Vice Mayor, Hon. Edgardo "Umpek" Sison, and the Sangguniang Bayan, reaffirming the municipality’s commitment to inclusive and gender-responsive programs for all sectors.
The activity was spearheaded by the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) under the leadership of Ms. Priscilla M. San Pascual, OIC–MGADO, together with the dedicated MGADO Team.
The discussion was enriched by Ms. Eleanor C. Mortiz, SAO, who served as the Resource Speaker and shared valuable insights on women’s rights and empowerment.
The office also extends its appreciation to Ms. Marjorie Engaño-Azcune, Jail Inspector and Acting Municipal Jail Warden, for her support and assistance in facilitating this meaningful activity.
Through this initiative, the Municipality of Rodriguez continues to uphold women’s rights, empowerment, and inclusive development, ensuring that no one is left behind.
#GAD
#WomensRights
#GenderSensitivity
#Empowerment
#InclusiveGovernance

LIBRENG SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGEMENT TRAINING PARA SA MGA KABABAIHAN NG MONTALBAN 
Naganap ngayong Lunes, Marso 23, 2026, ang unang araw ng libreng Social Media Management Training para sa mga kababaihan ng Montalban sa ilalim ng SPARK Technical Training Program ng Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) – Region IV-A. Ito'y isinasagawa sa pamamagitan ng ICT Literacy and Competency Development Bureau (ILCDB) ng DICT, katuwang ang Pamahalaang Bayan ng Montalban, sa pamumuno ni Mayor General Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret.), at sa pangangasiwa ng Municipal Livelihood Technical Vocational Education and Skills Development Center (MLTVESDC), Municipal Information Technology Office (MITO) at Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO).
Layunin ng pagsasanay na mapalawak ang kaalaman at kasanayan ng mga kalahok sa social media management, digital content creation, at online engagement upang mabigyan sila ng mas maraming oportunidad sa freelancing, online negosyo, at digital careers.
Ang apat na araw na face-to-face training ay dinaluhan ng 25 na piling kababaihang Montalbeño na handang matuto at makibahagi sa digital na mundo.
#BayanNgMontalban
#MGADO

Strengthening the Mainstreaming and Implementation of the HGDG of the MGFPS-EXECOM 
The Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) successfully conducted the activity “Strengthening the Mainstreaming and Implementation of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG)” for the Municipal GAD Focal Point System – Executive Committee (MGFPS-EXECOM) of the Municipality of Rodriguez, Rizal, held last March 17–19.
The three-day activity aimed to enhance the capacity of the MGFPS-EXECOM in integrating gender perspectives into planning, implementation, and evaluation of programs, projects, and activities, ensuring alignment with national GAD frameworks and policies.
This initiative was made possible through the continued support of Municipal Mayor, Hon. Gen. Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret.), Municipal Vice Mayor, Hon. Edgardo "Umpek" Sison, and the Sangguniang Bayan, particularly Committee on Gender and Development Chairperson, Hon. Arnel De Vera, and Vice Chairperson, Hon. Anne Cuerpo, reaffirming the municipality’s commitment to gender equality and inclusive governance.
The activity was spearheaded by the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) under the leadership of Ms. Priscilla M. San Pascual, OIC–MGADO, together with the dedicated MGADO Team.
Special recognition is given to Mr. Pent Elyria Dawn V. Liongson, Independent Gender Specialist and PCW Resource Pool, for sharing valuable expertise and guiding participants in strengthening gender-responsive planning and implementation.
Through this initiative, the Municipality of Rodriguez continues to advance effective GAD mainstreaming, ensuring that development efforts are inclusive, equitable, and responsive to the needs of all.
#HGDG
#GADMainstreaming
#MGFPS
#GenderEquality
#InclusiveGovernance
#RodriguezRizal

Capacity Building and Assessment on the Gender-Responsive LGU (GeRL) Tool for Barangay GAD Focal Point System (BGFPS) Members 
The Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO)* successfully conducted the Capacity Building and Assessment on the Gender-Responsive LGU (GeRL) Tool for Barangay GAD Focal Point System (BGFPS) Members, strengthening their knowledge and skills in assessing and enhancing gender responsiveness at the barangay level.
The activity aimed to equip participants with a deeper understanding of the GeRL Tool, enabling them to effectively evaluate governance, programs, and services through a gender lens and ensure alignment with national GAD mandates.
This initiative was made possible through the continued support of Municipal Mayor, Hon. Gen. Ronnie S. Evangelista (Ret.), Municipal Vice Mayor, Hon. Edgardo "Umpek" Sison, and the Sangguniang Bayan, particularly Committee on Gender and Development Chairperson, Hon. Arnel De Vera, and Vice Chairperson, Hon. Annee Cuerpo, reaffirming the municipality’s strong commitment to gender equality and inclusive governance.
The activity was spearheaded by the Municipal Gender and Development Office (MGADO) under the leadership of Ms. Priscilla M. San Pascual, OIC–MGADO, together with the dedicated MGADO Team, serving as the lead office in the successful implementation of the activity.
Special thanks to Ms. Olivia R. Cababat, PCW-GAD Resource Pool, for sharing her expertise and valuable insights that greatly contributed to the learning of the participants.
Through this initiative, the Municipality of Rodriguez continues to empower barangays in promoting gender-responsive governance, ensuring that programs and services are inclusive, equitable, and responsive to the needs of all.

#GenderResponsiveLGU
